action: Click the Start menu button (Windows icon) at the bottom left of the screen to open the Start menu, then type 'Notepad' in the search bar to search for the Notepad application.
fallback_action: Perform a Google search using Internet Explorer

recipe.steps:
is the first action. The task is to open the Start menu by clicking the Windows icon at the bottom left of the screen. The detected bounding box with the text 'Windows' (Box ID 74) is the Start menu button. I will click it to open the Start menu.
Action Description: Click the Windows Start menu button at the bottom left to open the Start menu.

previous action: The previous step was to click the Windows Start menu button (Box ID 74). The current screenshot shows the Start menu is now open, with a search bar visible at the top (Box ID 1) that says 'Search for apps, settings, and documents'. This confirms the Start menu is open and ready for input. The next step is to type 'Notepad' in the search bar to search for the Notepad application.
Action Description: Type 'Notepad' in the Start menu search bar to search for the Notepad application.

previous action: The previous step was to type 'Notepad' in the Start menu search bar. The current screenshot shows the Start menu with the search results for 'Notepad' visible. The Notepad application icon and name are clearly displayed, indicating that the search was successful. The next step is to open Notepad by clicking on its icon in the search results.
Action Description: Click the Notepad application icon in the Start menu search results to open Notepad.
